title: Laxmangarh Fort
tagline: Laxmangarh Fort — heritage city of Rajasthan

overview.short: Laxmangarh Fort (Hindi: लक्ष्मणगढ़ किला) is a ruined old fort on a hill in the town of Laxmangarh of Sikar district in the Indian state of Rajasthan.
overview.description: Laxmangarh Fort (Hindi: लक्ष्मणगढ़ किला) is a ruined old fort on a hill in the town of Laxmangarh of Sikar district in the Indian state of Rajasthan. Situated 30 kilometres (19 mi) from Sikar, it was built by Rao Raja Lakshman Singh of Sikar in 1805, who also founded a village in his own name as Laxmangarh in 1807. The most imposing building in this Laxmangarh town is its small fortress (owned by the Jhunjhunwala Family) which looms over the well laid out township on its west side. topPlaces.name: Laxmangarh
topPlaces.description: Laxmangarh is a town in the Sikar district of Rajasthan state in India.Note: This town should not be confused with Lachhmangarh, which is located in the Alwar district.

topPlaces.name: Bhojasar Bara, Sikar
topPlaces.description: Bhojasar Bara or Bara Bhojasar is a village within the Laxmangarh administrative region of Sikar district, Rajasthan, India. The village lies 23 kilometres (14 mi) north-west of Sikar and 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) south from Laxmangarh.

topPlaces.name: Khuri Bari
topPlaces.description: Khuri Bari is a village in Laxmangarh Tehsil, Sikar district in the Indian state of Rajasthan. As of 2011 Khuri Bari had a population of 2194 residents.

topPlaces.name: Bhojasar Chhota, Sikar
topPlaces.description: Bhojasar Chhota or Chhota Bhojasar is a village within the Laxmangarh administrative region of Sikar district, Rajasthan, India. The village lies 23 kilometres (14 mi) north-west of Sikar and 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) south from Laxmangarh.

items.note: Laxmangarh is a town in the Sikar district of Rajasthan state in India.Note: This town should not be confused with Lachhmangarh, which is located in the Alwar district.

Bhojasar Bara or Bara Bhojasar is a village within the Laxmangarh administrative region of Sikar district, Rajasthan, India. The village lies 23 kilometres (14 mi) north-west of Sikar and 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) south from Laxmangarh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Laxmangarh Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Laxmangarh Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Laxmangarh Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jaipur International Airport (136 km) and the nearest railway station is Jaipur Junction (126 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Laxmangarh Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Laxmangarh Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Laxmangarh Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Laxmangarh Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Laxmangarh Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Laxmangarh, Bhojasar Bara, Sikar, Khuri Bari, Bhojasar Chhota, Sikar.
